articles.title: THE AD INDUSTRY IS DUMPING YOUNGER WORKERS
articles.summary: Younger workers are rapidly disappearing from the advertising industry, with those aged 20-24 making up just 6.5% of the workforce in 2024, down from 10.5% in 2019. Unemployment among college graduates aged 22-27 has now surpassed the overall US jobless rate, a reversal of decades-long trends. Factors like economic uncertainty play a role, but AI is significantly reducing entry-level opportunities in marketing and advertising.

articles.title: YOUTUBE WILL START USING AI TO GUESS YOUR AGE
articles.summary: YouTube is rolling out an AI system to estimate users' ages and automatically apply teen safety measures for minors. If adults are misidentified as minors, they must verify their age with a government ID, credit card, or selfie, raising privacy concerns. The system uses viewing habits, searches, and account age to make determinations and currently applies only to logged-in users. This move follows broader industry efforts to prevent teens from bypassing age restrictions, but some users worry about sharing sensitive data.

articles.title: HOW TO USE DEEP RESEARCH FOR GTM
articles.summary: By combining good prompting with Deep Research, GTM teams can get detailed and actionable reports on projects such as marketing attribution, competitor analysis, or international expansion in a fraction of the time. The method works best when you provide business context and force the AI to use credible and recent sources. Different AI tools excel in different situations, with ChatGPT offering the most depth, Gemini providing balance, and Perplexity giving strong source control, making tool choice an important part of the strategy.

articles.title: SEO CHEAT CODES
articles.summary: Practical SEO strategies for startups include buying expired domains with backlinks and setting up 301 redirects, listing on high-DA sites for do-follow links, using Google Ads AI for keyword suggestions, and generating thousands of blog topics via tools like blogtopics.net and AnswerThePublic. Creating content around these questions builds topical authority. Diversifying content types, including programmatic SEO, free tools, blogs, and interconnected content rings, helps boost rankings.

articles.title: INTRODUCING THOUGHT LEADER EVENT ADS TO BUILD TRUST AND INFLUENCE
articles.summary: LinkedIn has introduced Thought Leader Event Ads, allowing advertisers to sponsor member posts that link directly to an event page. The update reflects the finding that 76% of B2B marketing leaders believe collaborating with creators builds brand authenticity, and 58% prioritize authenticity and credibility when selecting influencers. The ads are available globally and can be set up in Campaign Manager with member permission.

articles.title: WHAT SEARCH MARKETERS NEED TO KNOW ABOUT CHATGPT-5
articles.summary: ChatGPT-5 is faster, more accurate, and more expert-like. To appear in AI-driven answers, brands must be findable in search engines, cited across high-quality content, and present across multiple AI platforms. Brand mentions now matter more than backlinks, and content should be clear, structured, and semantically relevant. Keywords are less important than prompts, so build content around the questions real customers ask, creating a library of high-intent prompts.

articles.title: TOP 6 AI WRITING TOOLS TO HELP YOU WRITE SMARTER, NOT HARDER
articles.summary: This article reviews six AI writing tools, each with different strengths for academic, business, or creative work. StudyPro stands out for academic writing with built-in plagiarism checks, logical outlining, and context-aware generation, while Copy.ai excels at persuasive marketing copy, and AI-Writer focuses on source-based, cited content. Simplified offers design-friendly drafting, Buffer AI Tools handle quick polishing tasks, and Writer specializes in consistent, professional business writing.

articles.title: 10 AI-GENERATED AD EXAMPLES THAT'LL BLOW YOUR MIND
articles.summary: AI-generated ads are transforming marketing by giving brands the ability to produce original, personalized, and culturally resonant content faster and at a scale traditional methods cannot match. Campaigns from major companies show how AI can be applied to humor, emotional storytelling, cultural commentary, and even social good, often turning experimental ideas into global talking points. This post provides practical guidance on which AI tools can be used to replicate these successes with an emphasis on ethical oversight to protect authenticity and audience trust.

articles.title: I ANALYZED 1827 REAL USER PROMPTS FROM CHATGPT
articles.summary: ChatGPT users are shifting from keyword searches to detailed, task-focused conversational queries. Many involve coding help, hyper-local commercial requests, and persona-based instructions. This points to the rise of an agentic web where AI acts as a collaborator. This piece shares specific tactics for adapting, including structuring content and code for AI, creating brand personas for AI, and answering high-intent local queries. It also covers building resources like conversion guides, error glossaries, and comparison pages to capture opportunities in AI-driven search.

articles.title: WHO ARE YOUR REAL COMPETITORS?
articles.summary: Focus on competitors by asking what workarounds people use now, not just which startups offer similar solutions.

articles.title: SHOULD YOUR BRAND INVEST IN TIKTOK SHOP?
articles.summary: A flowchart of whether you should be investing in live shopping and social commerce.
